skill.name: data-engineer
skill.description: Helps design scalable data pipelines, data warehouses, lakehouse platforms, streaming systems, and governed analytics infrastructure.
skill.summary: Design reliable batch, streaming, warehouse, and lakehouse data platforms.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Reviewed 10 static matches in SKILL.md. The weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ordinary prose such as description and Design; the reconnaissance alert describes analytics engines, not host enumeration. No prompt injection, executable code, credential access, network exfiltration, or unsafe instructions were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Static Match
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography matches occur in ordinary documentation words such as description and design. No cryptographic algorithm, encryption implementation, hash selection, or security-sensitive code is present in these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each referenced line is prose about data engineering scope or architecture design. There is no code or instruction to use DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or another weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Static Match
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance match occurs on a line listing real-time analytics tools. It does not contain commands, host inspection, environment probing, or instructions to gather system details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a capability list naming Apache Pinot, ClickHouse, and Apache Druid. No system reconnaissance behavior or execution path is present.

content.value_statement: Data teams need pipelines that are reliable, governed, and cost aware. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design batch, streaming, warehouse, and lakehouse solutions.
actual_capabilities: Designs batch and streaming pipeline architectures.
Recommends storage, orchestration, and processing tools.
Plans data quality checks, lineage, governance, and monitoring.
Helps model warehouses with dimensional, data vault, and lakehouse patterns.
Reviews cost, performance, partitioning, clustering, and scaling tradeoffs.
limitations: Does not directly connect to private data platforms without user-provided access.
Does not execute pipelines or deploy cloud infrastructure by itself.
Requires schemas, volumes, latency goals, and compliance constraints for precise designs.
Tool versions and managed service features should be verified before implementation.

use_cases.title: Plan a Modern Data Platform
use_cases.description: Define storage, orchestration, transformation, governance, and monitoring choices for a scalable analytics platform.

use_cases.title: Improve Analytics Engineering Workflows
use_cases.description: Structure dbt models, data tests, lineage, and warehouse performance work for trusted reporting datasets.

use_cases.title: Design Real-Time Data Flows
use_cases.description: Compare Kafka, Flink, Kinesis, Pub/Sub, and warehouse sinks for event-driven analytics requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new data workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a batch data pipeline for these sources, destination, data volume, refresh schedule, and reporting needs.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Warehouse Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and Databricks for our workload, budget, governance needs, and team skills.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Streaming Lakehouse
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a streaming lakehouse architecture for event ingestion, schema evolution, quality checks, storage layout, and operational monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Governed Multi-Tenant Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed multi-tenant data platform for isolation, access control, lineage, compliance, cost, and failure recovery.

output_examples.input: Design a pipeline from application events to a BI-ready warehouse.
output: Architecture recommendation covering ingestion, processing, warehouse layout, and orchestration.
Data quality plan with validation points and alerting expectations.
Operational notes for monitoring, backfills, retention, and cost control.

output_examples.input: Review our dbt model strategy for monthly finance reporting.
output: Modeling feedback for staging, intermediate, and marts layers.
Testing suggestions for reconciliation, freshness, uniqueness, and accepted values.
Performance recommendations for partitioning, clustering, and incremental loading.

best_practices: Provide source schemas, expected volumes, freshness targets, and downstream consumers.
State compliance, PII, residency, and access control requirements before architecture selection.
Ask for operational plans that include monitoring, retries, backfills, and cost controls.
anti_patterns: Do not request a tool choice before defining latency, scale, and governance needs.
Do not skip data contracts, validation, or lineage for production pipelines.
Do not use one warehouse design for every workload without performance testing.

faq.question: Can this skill write production pipeline code?
faq.answer: It can help design and review implementation plans. Production code still needs repository context, credentials, tests, and deployment controls.

faq.question: Which data tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Spark, dbt, Airflow, Kafka, Flink, cloud warehouses, lakehouse formats, catalogs, and managed cloud data services.

faq.question: Can it help with data governance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for lineage, catalogs, data quality, access control, masking, audit logging, and compliance planning.

faq.question: Does it support real-time streaming designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can design event ingestion, stream processing, windowing, schema evolution, and real-time analytics architectures.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Provide source systems, schemas, data volumes, latency targets, cloud provider, existing tools, compliance needs, and failure tolerance.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for sensitive data work?
faq.answer: The skill gives safety-aware guidance, but users should avoid sharing secrets and should enforce their own access controls.
